title: JCE contract violation: session closed in doFinal prevents cipher reuse
problem: `releaseSession()` is called in the `finally` block of `engineDoFinal()`. Because `acquireSession()` is only called in `engineInit()`, subsequent `update()` or `doFinal()` operations on the same cipher instance will use `sessionHandle = 0`, leading to exceptions. The JCE `CipherSpi` contract requires instances to be reusable for multiple operations after a single `engineInit()`.

title: JCE contract violation: session closed in AEAD doFinal prevents cipher reuse
problem: `releaseSession()` is called in `doFinalAead()`, but `acquireSession()` is not called upon reentry, causing subsequent AEAD encryption/decryption operations on the reused cipher instance to fail with `sessionHandle = 0`.

title: JCE contract violation: session closed in doFinal prevents MAC reuse
problem: `engineDoFinal()` unconditionally releases the session handle. The standard Mac contract allows reuse of a Mac instance without reinitialization. The next time `engineDoFinal()` is invoked, `sessionHandle` will be `0`, causing the native invocation to fail.

title: JCE contract violation: session closed in doFinal prevents MAC reuse
problem: The Mac session is released in `engineDoFinal()`, invalidating the instance for subsequent operations that do not call `engineInit()`. Missing `acquireSession()` before doing native MAC operations.

title: Null sessionHandle crash during SM2Signature sign reuse
problem: `engineSign()` unconditionally calls `releaseSession()`. On a subsequent signing operation (reuse), `SM2Util.calculateE(sessionHandle, ...)` will be called with `sessionHandle = 0`, causing an exception before it even attempts native signing.

title: Null sessionHandle crash during SM2Signature verify reuse
problem: Similar to `engineSign()`, `engineVerify()` releases the session handle. If the signature instance is reused without reinitialization, `SM2Util.calculateE()` will be executed with `sessionHandle = 0`.
